relationship_cases.gold_rationale: Reversed direction: NVIDIA designs, TSMC fabricates; no source asserts NVIDIA fabricates wafers for TSMC.
Reversed direction of the Samsung foundry relation.
Reversed direction: NVIDIA buys memory from Micron, not the converse.
Reversed direction of the SK hynix memory relation.
Reversed direction: ASML supplies lithography systems to TSMC, not the converse.
Reversed direction: Intel is an ASML customer.
No source asserts NVIDIA supplies equipment to ASML.
Reversed direction: the 10-K license agreement is for Groq's LPU technology.
Type substitution: ASML makes lithography equipment; it does not fabricate wafers.
Type substitution: SK hynix supplies memory, not foundry services, per the cited passage.
Type substitution: Micron supplies memory.
Type substitution: TSMC is NVIDIA's foundry, not an equipment vendor.
No source asserts ASML supplies NVIDIA directly.
ASML's cited customers are Intel, Samsung and TSMC; NVIDIA is not asserted.
Type substitution: Hon Hai performs assembly and test, not wafer fabrication.
Groq appears in the 10-K as a license counterparty; it is not named in the competitors list passages.
Apple is not named in the 10-K competitors passages.
Meta is not named in the 10-K competitors passages.
Oracle is not named in the 10-K competitors passages.
Palantir is not named in the 10-K competitors passages.
Hedged disclosure: the 10-K states NVIDIA is finalizing an investment and partnership agreement with OpenAI with no assurance of completion; a completed investment is not asserted.
No acquisition of OpenAI is asserted anywhere in the corpus.
No NVIDIA investment in Anthropic is asserted in the corpus.
No Arm acquisition is asserted in the FY2026 10-K corpus.
The Groq agreement is a non-exclusive license, not an acquisition.
No source asserts TSMC fabricates for ASML.
No source in the frame asserts TSMC fabricates for Microsoft.
No source in the frame asserts TSMC fabricates for Apple.
Samsung is an ASML customer in the cited passage, not an equipment vendor to TSMC.
CoreWeave is not asserted as an NVIDIA supplier in the corpus.
The corpus does not assert NVIDIA purchases from CoreWeave.
Oracle is not asserted as an NVIDIA supplier.
Dell is not asserted as an NVIDIA supplier in the corpus.
Broadcom appears as a competitor, not as an NVIDIA supplier.
Qualcomm appears as a competitor, not as an NVIDIA supplier.
The 10-K names TSMC and Samsung as wafer foundries; Intel is named as a competitor.
Huawei appears as a competitor, not as an NVIDIA supplier.
Arm Holdings itself is not named as a competitor; the passage cites suppliers of Arm-based CPUs such as Amazon, Huawei, Microsoft.
No Arm-to-NVIDIA license is asserted in the corpus passages.
No Alphabet investment in NVIDIA is asserted.
No Microsoft investment in NVIDIA is asserted.

ASML is not asserted as an NVIDIA competitor.

Micron appears as a memory supplier, not a competitor.

No Tesla-buys-from-NVIDIA assertion in the frame.

Intel appears only in competitor context in the frame.
Capital Group appears only in a generic capital-availability passage; no investment relation is asserted.
Reversed direction of the hedged OpenAI disclosure; not asserted.

No xAI relation is asserted in the corpus.
